name: Muslim ibn Awsaja
title: The Loyal Companion
shortDescription: The honorable companion of the Prophet (PBUH) and the brave elder who was the first martyr among the Ansar in the mass attack.
fullStory:
Muslim ibn Awsaja al-Asadi was a prominent and respected figure in Kufa and a companion of the Prophet of Islam (PBUH). He was considered one of the sincere and devoted Shias of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS). During the uprising in Kufa, he was a main pillar of support for the Imam's ambassador, Muslim ibn Aqeel, and was responsible for collecting financial aid and purchasing weapons for the uprising. After Muslim's martyrdom, he managed to reach Imam Hussain's (AS) caravan in Karbala with great difficulty.

On the night of Ashura, when Imam Hussain (AS) lifted his allegiance from his companions and asked them to leave in the darkness of the night, Muslim ibn Awsaja was the first to rise and prove his loyalty with epic words. He said: 'Should we abandon you? What excuse would we have before God tomorrow for fulfilling your right? By God, I will not part from you until I break my spear in the chest of the enemy and strike them with my sword as long as I have life. If I have no weapon, I will throw stones at them until I am killed with you'.

According to the report in Sheikh Mufid's Al-Irshad, on the day of Ashura, Muslim ibn Awsaja was positioned in the left wing of the Imam's army under the command of Habib ibn Mazahir. When Amr ibn al-Hajjaj (commander of the right wing of the Kufan army) attacked the Imam's companions, a fierce battle ensued. In this mass attack, Muslim ibn Awsaja, despite his old age, fought heroically. After repelling the attack, he fell to the ground, having sustained heavy injuries.

When the dust settled, Imam Hussain (AS), along with Habib ibn Mazahir, rushed to his side while there was still a breath of life left in him. The Imam said: 'May God have mercy on you, O Muslim', and then recited the verse: 'Among them are those who have fulfilled their vow, and among them are those who await...'

Sheikh Mufid writes in Al-Irshad: Habib ibn Mazahir approached him and said: 'Your death is hard on me, but I give you the glad tidings of Paradise'. Habib then added: 'If I did not know that I would soon join you, I would have loved for you to give me your will'. Muslim ibn Awsaja, with a weak voice, pointing to Imam Hussain (AS), delivered his most historical will: 'I advise you regarding this man; fight for him until you die'. After that, he surrendered his soul to the Creator in the arms of the Imam.
quoteArabic: أُوصیکَ بِهذا رَحِمَکَ اللّهُ فَقاتِلْ دونَهُ حَتّی تَموتَ
quote: (In his last moments to Habib:) I advise you regarding this man (Imam Hussain); fight for him until you are killed.
